stories.headline: Israel intercepts Gaza flotilla near Crete and detains 175 activists
stories.summary: Pro-Palestinian activists say 22 boats carrying aid for Gaza were illegally intercepted in international waters near the Greek island.

stories.headline: Israel intercepts and detains crews of Gaza aid flotilla near Crete

stories.headline: Trump, Iran are locked in high-stakes standoff as oil prices hit 4-year high
stories.summary: As the U.S. blockade and Iranian mines and drones halt shipping in the Strait of Hormuz, U.S. gas prices also reached a level not seen since just after Russia’s Ukraine invasion.

stories.headline: Bank of England holds rates as Iran war sends oil prices to new highs
stories.summary: The Bank joins the Fed and Bank of Japan in sitting tight, as war in the Middle East drives oil prices higher and blows the spring rate-cut playbook apart.

stories.headline: Trump says US studying troop cuts in Germany, after Merz criticises war
stories.summary: His remarks come after the German chancellor criticised the US approach to the war in Iran.

stories.headline: Trump launches review of US troops in Germany
stories.summary: Threat comes days after German Chancellor Friedrich Merz criticised US strategy in Iran

stories.headline: Kyiv shuns Moscow's 'Victory Day truce' asking for long-term ceasefire
stories.summary: After the Easter truce, Moscow is now proposing a Victory Day parade ceasefire as the Kremlin is concerned over Ukraine’s long-range striking capabilities. Moscow has already scaled down the parade, where for the first time in years, Russian armoured columns will not be rolling on the Red Square.

stories.headline: Merz calls for a ‘a reliable transatlantic partnership’ after Trump threatens Germany with US troop reduction – Europe live
stories.summary: German chancellor stresses links with US and Nato during army visit without directly responding to president’s overnight commentsThe Commission was also asked about yesterday’s meeting of Hungary’s incoming prime minister, Péter Magyar.But we didn’t get much more than what we saw in yesterday’s social media posts from Magyar and the EU’s Ursula von der Leyen. stories.headline: Belgium seeks to take control of nuclear reactors in move to control energy supplies
stories.summary: Brussels believes the solution to soaring energy prices as a result of Russia's full-scale invasion of Ukraine and the war in the Middle East is to permanently transition away from fossil fuels.

stories.headline: Trump tightens US naval blockade as Iranian rial collapses to record low
stories.summary: The latest drop follows a currency shock several months ago, when the rial fell from around 1.4 million to 1.6 million per US dollar in under a week, as the war moves turns into an economic clash to exhaust the Iranian regime' options.

stories.headline: ‘Time to move forward, or be left behind’: Colombia climate talks end with fossil fuel phaseout push
stories.summary: Europe dominated the Santa Marta fossil fuel summit. Here are the key takeaways.

stories.headline: Israel intercepts Gaza-bound flotilla, arresting 175 activists
stories.summary: Isreal has intercepted a Gaza-bound aid flotilla in international waters near Greece, arresting 175 activists, with officials saying blockade enforcement is vital to stop weapons reaching the territory.

stories.headline: French growth stalls as Iran war energy shock hits economy
stories.summary: France's first-quarter data shows slowed growth, rising prices and collapsing exports, laying bare how quickly the Iran war's energy shock is feeding through to Europe's second-largest economy.

stories.headline: 'No Signal For Resolution' As US Mulls Iran Military Options
stories.summary: Brent oil rallied to a four-year high after Axios reported that US President Donald Trump will receive a briefing on new military options for action in Iran, signaling the potential for fresh escalation in the Middle East. The global benchmark surged as much as 7.1% before paring gains to trade near $122. Warren Patterson, Head of Commodities Strategy at ING Joined Stephen Carroll and Caroline Hepker on Bloomberg Radio To discuss. He says the mar

stories.headline: ECB Holds Rates as Impact of Iran War Unfolds
stories.summary: The European Central Bank kept interest rates unchanged at 2%, with officials signaling they need more time to assess the extent of the Iran war’s jolt to the economy. The ECB offered no guidance on future decisions, reiterating it will act one meeting at a time based on information as it arrives. Bloomberg's Oliver Crook reports from Frankfurt. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: In Blow to Voting Rights Act, Supreme Court Embraces Claim of Racial Progress
stories.summary: The majority said the law was a victim of its own success and no longer needed. Dissenters responded that Congress should make the call.

stories.headline: Trump Threatens to Pull Troops From Germany as He Lashes Out at Chancellor
stories.summary: The comments came after the German chancellor said that Iran has “humiliated” the United States.

stories.headline: Will the Iran war reshape the global energy order?
stories.summary: As oil markets shift, OPEC's grip is under pressure, US exports rise, and China drives the push towards renewables.

stories.headline: Press freedom worldwide falls to its lowest level in 25 years

stories.headline: US charges Mexican governor and other leaders with aiding drug cartel

stories.headline: Jewish agency warned of 'high' threat level ahead of Bondi shooting, report finds

stories.headline: Yen Jump Spurs Speculation of Intervention After ‘Final’ Warning
stories.summary: The yen surged as much as 3% as traders speculated Japan is intervening in the foreign-exchange market, hours after officials delivered a “final” warning to investors against selling the currency.

stories.headline: Bank of England Holds Rates as Officials Consider Hikes Ahead
stories.summary: The Bank of England kept interest rates on hold with several policymakers saying they might consider future hikes, just as oil prices soared within reach of the central bank’s most pessimistic scenario for the economy.

stories.headline: Janet Mills, Governor of Maine, Suspends Senate Campaign
stories.summary: Her withdrawal reflected the strength of her primary opponent, Graham Platner, as well as the mixed recruitment record of Chuck Schumer, the minority leader, as his party tries to reclaim the Senate.

stories.headline: Maine Gov. Janet Mills suspends Democratic Senate campaign
stories.summary: The seat, now held by Republican Sen. Susan Collins, is critical to Democrats’ hopes of retaking the Senate. Graham Platner, an oyster farmer and Democrat, is likely to win the primary.

stories.headline: Meta Looks to Raise as Much as $25 Billion With Jumbo Bond Sale

stories.headline: US Equity Futures Rise as Mega-Cap Earnings Lift Tech, Oil Drops
stories.summary: US stock futures advanced on Thursday after some mega-cap and other tech companies reported bullish earnings lifting their stocks, while oil prices fell from a four-year high.

stories.headline: OPEC+ Delegates Expect Another Symbolic Supply Hike Without UAE
stories.summary: OPEC+ is likely to agree another symbolic production increase for June, in the group’s first move since the surprise departure of the United Arab Emirates, three delegates said.

stories.headline: India’s Rupee Trims Losses After Record Low as Oil Rally Fades
stories.summary: India’s rupee slid to a record low on Thursday as a surge in crude prices rattled markets, before paring most of its losses after oil retreated from a four-year high.

stories.headline: Russia scales back Moscow Victory Day parade, blaming threat from Ukraine
stories.summary: Russia says the annual commemoration of Soviet victory in World War Two will not feature military vehicles or cadets.

stories.headline: The 'Polar Bear Capital' with Arctic gateway ambitions
stories.summary: Canada is weighing whether a year-round northern trade route to Europe from Churchill, Manitoba, is viable, as climate change speeds Arctic warming.

stories.headline: Global forest loss slows but El Niño fires could threaten progress
stories.summary: The loss of tropical rainforests eased last year, according to new analysis, but scientists warn they are still disappearing rapidly.

stories.headline: Supreme Court limits key provision of the landmark Voting Rights Act
stories.summary: The decision could touch off a scramble by Republicans to redraw majority-minority congressional districts, especially in the South, that could cost many Black Democrats their seats.

stories.headline: What the Supreme Court did to the Voting Rights Act

stories.summary: Signs of economic resilience and a batch of solid corporate earnings fueled a rebound in stocks, which also gained as oil retreated from a four-year high.

stories.headline: Indian billionaire's son offers to save Escobar's hippos
stories.summary: Anant Ambani - son of Asia's richest man Mukesh Ambani - says he can take care of the animals roaming free since the drug lord's death.

stories.headline: Japan Airlines trials humanoid robots as ground handlers
stories.summary: These robots may in future help clean cabins and operate ground support equipment.

stories.summary: The Army officer has been accused of using classified information to win $400,000 on an online prediction market.

stories.summary: Officials say a rebel leader known as "Marlon" ordered the attack in which 20 people were killed.

stories.summary: The Israeli PM's directive comes as six people were killed by strikes in Lebanon despite a ceasefire being extended by three weeks.

stories.headline: BBC visits Chernobyl ghost city 40 years after world's worst nuclear accident
stories.summary: The BBC's Jessica Parker visits Pripyat, which was abandoned in 1986 after an explosion at the nearby Chernobyl nuclear power plant.

stories.headline: Katya Adler: Europe's Nato allies push back at reported US threat to Spain
stories.summary: Souring relations between Europe and the US have reared their Medusa-like head again, writes the BBC's Europe editor.
